70.reason: [Error: Only spaces are handled as word separators. Non‑letter characters (digits, punctuation) are ignored, causing letters from adjacent tokens to be concatenated (e.g., "since" + "c" → "sincec"). Should treat any non‑letter as a word boundary and only create word when j>0.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Only spaces are handled as word separators. Non‑letter characters (digits, punctuation) are ignored, causing letters from adjacent tokens to be concatenated (e.g., "since" + "c" → "sincec"). Should treat any non‑letter as a word boundary and only create word when j>0.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Only space terminates a word; any non‑letter character should terminate, e.g., periods and digits are ignored, causing concatenation like "sincec"]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition only treats space as a delimiter. It fails to treat other non-letter characters (like digits, punctuation, newlines) as delimiters, causing words to merge incorrectly (e.g., "since" and "c" from "since1972.c" might get merged or handled improperly if not separated by space). The problem states a word is only letters, so any non-letter should break the word.
